task_completion.description: The VeraCrypt main window is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The standard VeraCrypt GUI window is displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Two encrypted volumes are initially shown as mounted in the slot list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VeraCrypt slot list displays exactly two entries with mounted volumes (e.g., test_volume.hc and mounted_volume.hc) before the agent takes action.

task_completion.description: The agent locates and targets the 'Dismount All' button.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is positioned over the 'Dismount All' button in the VeraCrypt main window.

task_completion.description: The 'Dismount All' button is clicked to unmount all volumes at once.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual indication of a click on the 'Dismount All' button, such as the button appearing depressed, highlighted, or the immediate subsequent clearing of the slot list.

task_completion.description: After dismounting, the slot list shows no mounted volumes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VeraCrypt main window's slot list is completely empty, with no file paths or volume names listed next to any of the drive letters.

integrity.description: The agent used the VeraCrypt GUI to dismount the volumes, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows visible executing commands like 'veracrypt -d' to unmount the drives.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual VeraCrypt application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface used must be the genuine VeraCrypt GUI, not a text editor, mockup, or file manager.

integrity.description: The agent used the 'Dismount All' button rather than dismounting volumes individually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the single 'Dismount All' button rather than selecting individual slots and clicking 'Dismount' multiple times.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task starts with exactly 2 volumes mounted (test_volume.hc and mounted_volume.hc). The verifier expects the final state to have 0 volumes mounted. Confirm that the slot list in the VeraCrypt GUI is completely empty at the end of the task.
